mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reflects an accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS fell from 5.25% in Sep 2007 to 0.97% in Oct 2008
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following months with lower FEDFUNDS is not positive on average over any 3-year rolling window, or the t-statistic of the mean excess return is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration that erodes consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reached 6.5% in October 2008 up from 4.7% a year prior
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE is above 6.0% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) reflect a contracting consumption-labor feedback loop where falling incomes reduce spending and vice versa, amplifying downside pressure on corporate revenues and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS declining and UNRATE rising through Q4 2008
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to produce a Spearman correlation below -0.05 with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods marked by federal funds rates below 2 percent, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) generate acute capacity underutilization and margin compression that force negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO falling in late 2008 while FEDFUNDS at 0.97%
regime_hint: recession with policy rates below 2 percent
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2%, month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 900 thousand units mark a prolonged contraction in residential investment and durable goods demand, reducing construction employment and mortgage credit expansion, which drags S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 817 in Sep 2008 down from over 2000 in 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 900 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.5 percent, simultaneous month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL create a stagflationary environment where easy monetary policy fails to stimulate real output but erodes real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% in Oct 2008 with CPI still elevated
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to achieve a t-statistic magnitude greater than 2.0 in predicting negative 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and operating leverage that translate into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth does not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns at a Spearman rank correlation above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points reflects a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that precedes downward earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE jumped from 6.1% in Sep 2008 to 6.5% in Oct 2008
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 4-year rolling window.
